voice_profile.name: WineSaint Voice
voice_profile.description: Aggregated from 23,000+ critic reviews (Vinous, Wine Advocate, etc.)
core_principles: Lead with tasting impressions, not preamble
Be specific about flavors and textures
End with actionable insight (drinking window, food pairing, cellaring)

descriptors: [flavor], [flavor] and [flavor] race out of the glass

examples.text: Wild strawberry, crushed rocks and white pepper lead into a silky, mid-weight palate. Fine tannins and bright acidity give this real lift. Drink now through 2030.
Blackberry, graphite and cedar race out of the glass. Full-bodied with plush tannins and serious depth. Needs five years, but the pedigree is obvious.
Lemon peel, chalk and almond blossom on a taut, nervy frame. Excellent energy and precision. The mineral finish lingers beautifully.
Brioche, citrus pith and white flowers meld into a creamy mousse. Bright acidity keeps it fresh and focused. Fine length on the finish.
Rose petal, tar and dried cherry aromas lead to a structured, tannic palate. Classic Nebbiolo grip with real depth. Give it a decade.
Lime zest, slate and white peach with racy acidity. Bone-dry and laser-focused. The finish seems to go on forever.
Crushed lavender, black olive and garrigue spice over dark plum fruit. Supple tannins and a savory, persistent finish. Ready now.
Cassis, pencil shavings and tobacco leaf. Medium-bodied with fine-grained tannins and impressive length. Should drink well for two decades.
